old_claim.note: Gothic Architecture provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct Gothic architecture dependency remains because concentric castles are scoped to layered fortification walls and overlapping defense, not ecclesiastical Gothic design.

source_shape.source_locator: English Heritage discusses castle development through defensive form and castle types; the cited scope does not make Gothic architecture a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source grounds the node in castle fortification development, not in Gothic architectural style.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Gothic architecture can coexist chronologically with castles, but the direct dependency should stay on construction, masonry, siege, and feudal fortification context.

ontology_before: Concentric Castles depended on Gothic Architecture as an unsourced enabling edge.
ontology_after: Concentric Castles keeps construction, siege engineering, masonry, and feudal context without importing Gothic style as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: gothic_architecture is no longer a direct prerequisite for concentric_castles.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to Gothic castle ornament or ecclesiastical castle architecture.
A stronger source showed concentric castle design directly derived from Gothic architectural methods.
A separate gothic_castle_style node were added and intentionally linked to Gothic Architecture.
short_reason: Gothic style is not the fortification mechanism.
